name: AWS WAF Admin Surface Restriction (CIDR Allowlist)
objective: Restrict access to sensitive administrative paths (for example /admin, /wp-admin, and /manage) so only approved source CIDRs can reach them.
description: This pack enforces a path-based administrative surface restriction policy in AWS WAF. Requests to known admin endpoints are allowed only when they originate from trusted source CIDR ranges. Non-allowlisted sources are blocked (or counted during rollout). This reduces exposure of high-value interfaces to opportunistic scanning, credential stuffing, and targeted exploitation.
risk_mitigated: Credential stuffing against admin login paths
Internet-wide scanner access to privileged endpoints
false_positive_tuning_notes: Start in COUNT mode and review sampled requests for at least 7 days before BLOCK.
Validate all legitimate administrator egress IPs (VPN, office, bastion, SASE, break-glass networks) are present in the allowlist.
If administrators are mobile or use rotating residential IPs, front admin access through a fixed egress (VPN/ZTNA) before enforcing BLOCK.
Tune path matching for your stack. Include exact paths and prefixes used by your framework/admin tooling.
Exclude health checks or internal probes if they hit restricted paths from non-allowlisted ranges.
rollout_guidance: Phase 1 (observe): Deploy with rule action COUNT on non-allowlisted admin-path requests.
Phase 2 (verify): Confirm expected admin traffic is allowlisted and no critical workflows are impacted.
Phase 3 (enforce): Switch action to BLOCK and monitor blocked request metrics and sampled logs.
Phase 4 (harden): Narrow allowed CIDRs further and add alerting for repeated blocked attempts.

rules.logic_summary: If URI path starts with a restricted admin path AND source IP is NOT in approved CIDR IP set, then COUNT (initial) or BLOCK (enforced).

terraform_example.description: Attach a staged admin-surface restriction rule to an AWS WAFv2 WebACL. Deploy COUNT first, then flip to BLOCK after validation.

deployment_notes: Use REGIONAL scope for ALB/API Gateway/App Runner and CLOUDFRONT scope for CloudFront distributions.
Ensure rule priority places this control before broad allow rules that might otherwise bypass intent.
Enable sampled requests and CloudWatch metrics to validate impact before enforcement.
